evidence_quotes.quote: consumer protection against companies shutting down their online games we paid for as a product (not service).
a game which a lot of us purchased can no longer be played because Ubisoft shut down their servers and made a purchased product unplayable.

paragraphs.text: Please note that my name - which I cannot edit when I log in via Google - is not Zathrus Writer but a Google nickname I chose when I created the account. My real name is Martin Ambrus. Thank you. Digital Fairness Act sounds like an amazing initiative to include one additional point - consumer protection against companies shutting down their online games we paid for as a product (not service). We recently saw this with The Crew as a great example - a game which a lot of us purchased can no longer be played because Ubisoft shut down their servers and made a purchased product unplayable. There is a growing number of people who start noticing this and reacting - a good example being the "Stop Killing Games" initiative EU petition, which by now has almost 1,5 million signatures. I would vouch for adding the point of companies to stop "killing" games (products) we purchased into this initiative, as a possible resolution could not only help citizens of EU (and potentially on the whole Earth) but also increase the popularity of European Union and its lawmaking process as a whole. That - in my eyes - is a win-win for everyone.
